# sentry_monitor (Data Source)

⚠️ This resource is currently in beta and may be subject to change. It is supported by [New Monitors and Alerts](https://docs.sentry.io/product/new-monitors-and-alerts/) and may not be viewable in the UI today.

Retrieve a monitor by ID, or by project and type.

When you create a new project, Sentry automatically creates [default monitors](https://docs.sentry.io/product/new-monitors-and-alerts/monitors/#default-monitors). Use the `project` and `type` attributes to retrieve them. The monitor ID can then be used to set up an [alert rule for the monitor](../resources/alert.md).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Issue Stream Monitor: The default monitor tracking new issues of all types created for a project
data "sentry_monitor" "issue_stream" {
  organization = "my-org"     # Or Organization ID
  project      = "my-project" # Or Project ID

  type = "issue_stream"
}

output "issue_stream_monitor_id" {
  value = data.sentry_monitor.issue_stream.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization` (String) The organization slug or internal ID of the monitor.

### Optional

- `first` (Boolean) Return the first monitor found.
- `id` (String) The internal ID of the monitor to retrieve. Conflicts with `project`, `type`, and `first`.
- `project` (String) The project slug or internal ID of the monitor. Conflicts with `id`.
- `type` (String) Monitor type to retrieve.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) The description of the monitor.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the monitor is enabled.
- `name` (String) The name of the monitor.
- `owner` (Attributes) Sentry will assign new issues to this assignee. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--owner))

<a id="nestedatt--owner"></a>
### Nested Schema for `owner`

Read-Only:

- `team_id` (String) The team internal ID to assign new issues to.
- `user_id` (String) The user internal ID to assign new issues to.
